name: Cahp
description_short: California Highway Patrol Credit Union serves peace officers and their families with lending, deposit, and wealth management services through a member-owned, not-for-profit structure.
description_long:
California Highway Patrol Credit Union (CAHP) is a specialized credit union established to serve California peace officers and their immediate families. As a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution insured by the NCUA, CAHP operates with a mission to provide exclusive financial solutions tailored to those in law enforcement. The organization positions itself as a trusted financial partner committed to understanding the unique needs of peace officers and supporting their financial well-being.

CAHP offers a comprehensive suite of lending products including home loans, vehicle loans, debt-consolidation loans, and personal loans. The credit union emphasizes competitive, low rates across its lending offerings and positions itself as a full-service lender. Beyond traditional lending, CAHP provides wealth management and investment advisory services in partnership with Integrity Financial Corporation, an independent registered investment advisor. This allows members to access personalized financial planning, investment strategies, and comprehensive wealth management solutions.

What distinguishes CAHP is its exclusive membership focus on California peace officers and their families, creating a community-based financial institution tailored to a specific professional demographic. The partnership with Integrity Financial Corporation for wealth management services elevates CAHP beyond basic credit union offerings into comprehensive financial advisory territory. The organization emphasizes relationship-based banking with a commitment to helping members improve credit scores and maintain financial well-being.

CAHP is a legitimate credit union serving a defined membership base with standard lending and deposit services. However, potential members should note that membership is restricted to eligible peace officers and family members—not open to the general public. As with any investment advisory relationship, members should review Integrity Financial's Form ADV Part 2 and understand that investment services carry inherent market risks, and past performance does not guarantee future results.
services: Full-service banking for peace officers and families
pros: Exclusive focus on peace officers and families with specialized understanding of their financial needs
Full-service lender offering competitive rates on home, auto, debt consolidation, and personal loans
Access to professional wealth management and investment advisory services through Integrity Financial Corporation
Credit union structure (not-for-profit, member-owned) prioritizes member benefits over shareholder profits
NCUA insurance protection on member deposits and accounts
Personalized financial planning and advisory services with dedicated advisors
Emphasis on helping members improve credit scores and achieve financial well-being
cons: Membership is restricted to California peace officers and their families—not open to general public
Investment advisory services are provided by third-party (Integrity Financial Corporation), not directly by CAHP
Website does not clearly specify current interest rates, terms, or loan limits for any products
Limited information about deposit products, checking/savings account features, or share certificate offerings
No mention of online banking capabilities, mobile app, or digital service offerings
best_for: California peace officers seeking specialized lending with competitive rates and community understanding
Law enforcement professionals interested in comprehensive wealth management and investment planning
Peace officer families needing debt consolidation or major purchases (home, vehicle) at favorable rates
Members of law enforcement seeking credit-building and financial well-being support from a trusted institution
diagnosis: CAHP Credit Union is best for California peace officers and their families seeking relationship-based banking with competitive lending rates and professional wealth management guidance. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to eligible peace officers and immediate family members—this is not a credit union open to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
